objective: Define a control-oriented asset model that formalizes singular authority, promotion, runtime projection, and assurance for critical repo and runtime surfaces, then operationalize that model through a structured $git_root/.control bundle with authored JSON control objects, CUE assertions, and generated validation reports.

in: per-asset authority chain from proposal gate to rollback shape
hard separation of policy, contract, and runtime planes
runtime source versus runtime projection modeling
assurance trigger, severity, and action classification
the .control bundle as the operationalized control surface for a git root
self-governance rules for the control bundle itself
initial scoped assets: session_context, prompt seeds and composed prompt, service-assurance triggers, git policy surface, and skill registry lifecycle
out: runtime implementation of spawn assurance or prompt composition
replacing JSON Schema as canonical leaf contract authority
granting workflow skills ownership of global policy surfaces
broad mutation from .path triggers without prior policy and contract definition
full host rollout beyond the initial scoped assets

entities.name: asset_control_entry
entities.role: one controlled asset with singular authority, promotion, runtime, and assurance metadata

entities.name: control_bundle
entities.role: the .control directory containing authored control objects, assertions, and generated reports

entities.name: control_policy
entities.role: policy-plane object defining norms, allowed operations, and enforcement classes

entities.name: control_contracts
entities.role: contract-plane object defining controlled assets, promotion semantics, and schema references

entities.name: control_runtime
entities.role: runtime-plane object defining projections, triggers, assurance checks, and rollback behavior

entities.name: control_assertions
entities.role: CUE assertion layer that validates cross-object consistency without becoming the primary source of truth

entities.name: control_report
entities.role: generated validation and drift report emitted by the wrapped validator

constraints: each controlled asset must have one singular authority owner and one explicit authority chain
policy, contract, and runtime are the only primary planes in v1; secondary labels must be expressed as concerns
runtime projection must be distinguished from runtime source for every controlled asset
authored control objects and generated control reports must be explicitly separated
promotion semantics must be represented in addition to promotion paths
the .control bundle must govern itself and must not become a self-exempt escape hatch
JSON Schema remains canonical for control object validity, while CUE assertions validate cross-object admissibility and consistency
spawnctl owns wrapped validation in v1; validation must occur before assurance actions
service assurance triggers must default to report/reconcile semantics and must not perform broad mutation unless pre-approved and deterministic
initial scoped assets must be listed explicitly, and session_context must be fully populated as the first control exemplar before later assets graduate into fully populated control entries
any root-level ACM extension must explicitly separate live-fact mutation from promotion-gated fidelity review before defining current and delta artifact families
any root-level ACM extension must freeze root-authority precedence and controller conflict resolution before drafting root-scoped schemas
any root-level ACM extension that defines current or delta artifact families must also define recomputation, tombstone, idempotence, ordering, and partial-failure behavior
acceptance_criteria: the proposal defines the only primary planes and separates secondary concerns from plane assignment
the proposal freezes the minimum asset_control_entry field set required for operational control
the proposal defines the .control authored/generated bundle inventory explicitly
the proposal pins validator ownership, severity classes, exit-code behavior, and report emission contract
the proposal names session_context as the first exemplar asset and fully populates its control entry without broadening into runtime implementation
open_questions: whether runtime should consume a promoted projection instead of the live worktree for all high-drift assets in v1
which of the remaining initially scoped assets should receive the second fully populated control entry after session_context

plane_model.rule: Use only primary_planes for top-level plane assignment. Put secondary labels in concerns.

control_bundle_v1.rule: The .control bundle is structured and versioned; generated reports are evidence only and not authority surfaces.

first_exemplar_asset.why: session_context is the first fully proposal-gated control exemplar and should be used to prove the asset-control model before broader rollout.

initial_scoped_assets.population_rule: Each initial scoped asset must be explicitly listed. session_context is fully populated in v1 as the first control exemplar; the remaining scoped assets are declared now and require follow-on packets before they may graduate to fully populated control entries.

control_entry.promotion_method: proposal-gated packet acceptance followed by review-clean contract publication
promotion_preconditions: worker_packets session_context schema and semantic regression fixtures remain green
runtime reconciliation continues to treat live thread authority as non-mutable in place
control_entry.runtime_consumer: spawn session-context reconciler, fresh-session bootstrap, and downstream artifact consumers such as daily-reports
control_entry.runtime_source: spawn-owned authoritative operational session context, with ~/.config/codex/sessions and ~/.local/state/codex/meta/effective_prompt.txt treated as candidate inputs to reconciliation
control_entry.projection_mutability: spawn-authoritative projection; current state is atomically replaced, evidence directories are append-only, and on-disk edits remain candidate inputs only
assurance_trigger: timer: proposal-pinned scheduled reconciliation trigger for the next spawn implementation slice
assurance_checks: validate the session_context contract family and runtime semantic rules before accepting projected state
emit session_context.sync_event.v1 for every reconcile request
emit fresh_session_loader_report.v1 for every fresh-session load, including no-op and failure cases
preserve the authority boundary that forbids in-place mutation of live thread authority
control_entry.rollback_shape: restore the last accepted CODEX_STATE/session_context/current/session_context.v1.json and latest.json pointer while preserving append-only sync_event and loader_report evidence
notes: the current operational refresh baseline already exists through spawnctl codex-session refresh and the spawnd session refresh units
this exemplar formalizes that baseline for control assessment and does not introduce a parallel refresh mechanism

assets.why: Prompt seeds and the composed prompt are already operationally tied to session refresh and should be assessed immediately after the session_context exemplar is accepted.
Service assurance triggers govern when report/reconcile behavior fires and must be modeled explicitly before broader automatic mutation is allowed.
Git policy defines promotion and rollback boundaries for controlled assets and is part of the first operational control perimeter.
Skill registry lifecycle affects tool and skill availability and should be controlled after session_context proves the model.
